items.topic: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses - TechCrunch
items.script: Hey everyone, and welcome to Elixpo Daily! Get ready, because the future of wearables might just be getting a whole lot more stylish and intelligent. Apple is reportedly deep in testing no fewer than four distinct designs for its upcoming smart glasses, rumored to be aiming for a 2027 launch. We're talking everything from big rectangular frames to slimmer ones that might remind you of Tim Cook's own glasses, plus a couple of oval and circular options in shades like ocean blue and light brown. Now, here’s the twist: these aren't Vision Pro clones. Instead, they’re focusing on AI-powered magic – snapping photos and videos with those cool oval lenses, taking calls, playing music, and unleashing an upgraded Siri to interact with your world. It looks like Apple is really pushing to compete with Meta’s Ray-Ban smart glasses, and they’re even developing special low-power chips to keep things running smoothly. The race is on to see what Apple reveals next, but it seems the real game-changer is just around the corner. Speaking of things on the horizon, NASA already has next Artemis flight in its sights following astronauts’ triumphant moon flyby.
